hazyresearch/thunderkittens.note: AMD counterpart by same authors: github.com/HazyResearch/HipKittens

heavyai/heavydb.note: GPU queries run via a runtime LLVM NVVM->NVPTX codegen pipeline (NativeCodegen.cpp lookupTarget(nvptx64)/triple nvptx64-nvidia-cuda/nvvm.annotations/libdevice) JIT-linked to cubin by the CUDA driver (NvidiaKernel.cpp cuLink*) and launched via the CUDA driver API. No static GPU kernel to translate and no isolated GPU unit test -> no correctness-first slice. A ROCm port needs a new LLVM-AMDGPU codegen backend + ROCm device-libs link + hipRTC/comgr runtime-compile + a port of ~70 CUDA-driver-coupled files and the warpcore CG hash table: upstream-engineering scale. The analogous Intel SPIR-V+Level-Zero backend (PRs 671/683/688-691/697) was attempted and closed unmerged; only an inert L0 memory-manager stub remains. Not a tractable mechanical MOAT port; not GPU-validatable in-pipeline. See projects/heavydb/plan.md.

The open Apache-2.0 repo contains only Python frontends. A ROCm port would require greenfield kernel reimplementation, not CUDA->HIP translation.

shi-labs/natten.note: libnatten is 100% CUTLASS/CuTe (Ampere/xFormers FNA+FMHA, Hopper wgmma/TMA, Blackwell); naive & CPU CUDA kernels deleted upstream (CHANGELOG: 'Dropped unfused / CPU backends in libnatten'; na{1,2,3}d_{qk,qkrpb,av} removed). CUTLASS does not port to ROCm per PORTING_GUIDE; no non-CUTLASS GPU kernel remains as a first slice. AMD already works via the Flex (torch.nn.attention.flex_attention -> Triton) backend, which is explicitly ROCm-aware (checks.py is_rocm).
